---
title: "Tornadoes on the Fried chicken chef states"
description: "Tornadoes in the US states that form a shape of a Chef with a plate of fried chicken."
date: "2026-03-05"
image: "../images/tornados-kfc.jpg"
categories: [R, Tidytuesday, map, Barchart]
toc: true
---

![](../images/tornados-kfc.jpg){.project-hero-img fig-alt="Tornadoes in the US states that form a shape of a Chef with a plate of fried chicken."}

::: {.project-meta}
::: {.meta-item}
📅 March 2026
:::
::: {.meta-item}
🛠️ R, ggplot2, dplyr
:::
::: {.meta-item}
📊 [Data source](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2023/2023-05-16/readme.md)
<!-- ::: -->
<!-- ::: {.meta-item} -->
<!-- 💻 [Code on GitHub](https://github.com/yourusername/repo) -->
:::
:::

## What & Why

The dataset contained a historical overview of tornadoes in the US and an meme
came to memory where you could see that the states of Minnesota, Iowa, Missouri,
Kentucky, Arkansas, Tennessee and Louisiana formed the silhouette of a chef with
a plate of food on their hands. Since the food corresponds to the state of Kentucky,
it made sense to think of it as fried chicken.
I was also curious to see if the incidence of tornadoes had increase with time
and it does appear so.

## Approach

You can see the aforementioned chef and next to each of the states, a barchart of 
number of tornadoes. Moreover, for higher magnitude tornadoes, a line is drawned
from the coordinates where it started using color for magnitude and length for the
miles it traveled.
